title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to its first NCAA Division II World Series after defeating Molloy University in a three-game series.
Endicott College reached its fourth consecutive NCAA Division III championship round following a Super Regional win over Salve Regina.
Bentley and Endicott will compete in North Carolina and Ohio respectively, with opponents and schedules yet to be determined.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics plan to work out UConn star Alex Karaban ahead of 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban ranks No. 32 on ESPN's draft prospects; Boston holds No. 27 and No. 40 picks.
Karaban leaves UConn as its winningest player, with 1,880 points and 126 victories.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway as part of his 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour' on May 22, 2026.
The Berklee alum blended jazz and pop while including new tracks and older hits, engaging the audience deeply.
Opening acts included Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey, who captivated the crowd with strong vocal performances and musical versatility.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' album in full on May 22, 2026, at The Cabot in Beverly.
The concert also featured the Wallflowers covering Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album, highlighting their appreciation for classic rock.
Highlights included performances of 'One Headlight' and a cover of Petty's 'You Got Lucky', drawing enthusiastic audience responses.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston experienced the largest population decline in Massachusetts last year, losing 1,338 residents, according to U.S. Census data.
Everett and Worcester gained the most residents, adding 2,362 and 1,916 people respectively, out of all Massachusetts communities.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 people last year, a 0.2% increase, significantly slower than previous years, per Census data.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Market Basket's parent company featured in Forbes' largest family businesses list.
Fidelity, owned by the Johnson family, ranked 14th with $37.7 billion in annual revenue.
Demoulas Super Markets, with $8 billion revenue, ranked 69th and is fully owned by the Demoulas family.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities to overhaul campus housing, addressing student needs and affordability.
The phased redevelopment plans include modernizing residential, dining, and retail amenities while students remain on campus.
The project aligns with Massachusetts's broader efforts to address housing shortages and promote sustainability and community wellbeing.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at the Chevalier Theatre in Medford, showcasing protest songs from her new album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
The concert included a mix of older hits and politically charged tracks, with Williams addressing political themes in her speeches.
Williams, unable to play guitar due to a previous stroke, led her band through covers and her own tracks with energetic performances.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Justin Slaten allowed four runs in less than an inning during a loss to the Twins.
Boston went to their bullpen in the seventh inning, but the move backfired, leading to an 8-6 defeat.
Despite a strong start with nine strikeouts, Payton Tolle was removed after six innings at 85 pitches.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens, former Red Sox pitcher, threw the first pitch at Fenway Park before the game against the Minnesota Twins.
Clemens's pitch was caught by his son, Kody Clemens, who plays for the Twins as a position player.
Red Sox fans received Roger Clemens bobbleheads celebrating his 20-strikeout games in 1986 and 1996 during the event.
